evals.prompt: Write markup for a decorative divider image.
evals.expected_output: Uses an empty alt attribute so the decorative image is ignored by assistive technology.
expectations: The decorative image has empty alt text
The image is not announced to screen readers
The image does not carry meaning
The markup explicitly marks it decorative by omission from the accessibility tree

evals.prompt: Write alt text for a functional image used as the only content of a link.
evals.expected_output: Describes the destination or action rather than the visual content.
expectations: The image alternative names the action or destination
The link remains understandable without the image
The alt text is not just a file description
The accessible name is specific enough for screen readers

evals.prompt: Write alt text and nearby description for a complex chart image.
evals.expected_output: Uses a short alt summary plus an adjacent long description for the detailed data.
expectations: The chart has a concise alt summary
The chart also has a longer adjacent description
The description includes the key data points
The image is not left with summary-only text

evals.prompt: Write alt text for an image of text in a sale banner.
evals.expected_output: Repeats the exact visible text rather than describing the banner visually.
expectations: The alt text matches the visible text
The text in the image is reproduced for AT users
The alt text does not describe the image as a banner
The message is conveyed without visual context

evals.prompt: Write markup for a star rating made from several images.
evals.expected_output: Gives meaningful alt text to the first star and empty alt to the repeated decorative stars.
expectations: The group has one meaningful alternative
Repeated stars are ignored by AT
The rating is understandable from the accessible text
The pattern avoids duplicate announcements
